reasoning: Without search tools to gather time-specific information for 2025-11-09, I must rely on a base-rate random walk model for stock prices. Generally, stocks have a slight upward drift over time, pushing the probability of being higher than a current value slightly above 50%. However, HST is a REIT and typically pays regular quarterly dividends. Over a 3-month horizon (into February 2026), an ex-dividend date is extremely likely (typically in late December), which will mechanically reduce the stock price by the dividend amount, offsetting some or all of the expected upward drift. Furthermore, as per the forecasting instructions, uncertainty tends to increase over time in the absence of a stable known trend, pushing probabilities for more distant dates closer to 0.5. Therefore, I assign 52% to the shortest date (relying slightly on standard equity risk premium drift over a short horizon before dividend payout) and decay it to 50% for the longest horizon, reflecting maximum uncertainty and the anticipated dividend offset.
